text: The surgeon performs an inferior vena cava dissection through transperitoneal access after detaching the right colic. The dissection is associated with the genital vein, providing visual access to the anastomosis of the contralateral left renal vein.
The surgeon mobilizes the liver and divides the hepatic veins to enhance exposure of the surgical field.
The surgeon progressively dissects the left renal vein on each side, using a loop to bring it out for better visualization and manipulation.
The surgeon performs renal arterial dissection and clamping through an interaortical caval access, positioned on both sides of the left renal vein.
The surgeon completes the dissection and division of the right renal vein, exposing the large adenopathy adjacent to the vein.
The surgeon detaches retrocaval lymph nodes step by step, starting with the anterior face, using a retro- and interaorticocaval axis approach.
The surgeon performs meticulous lymphostasis using hemostatic clips, ensuring control of lymphatic vessels in the surgical field.
The surgeon identifies and divides the ureter, visually separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon detaches the kidney at its inferior pole, along with the surrounding perirenal fat and Gerota's fascia, using careful dissection techniques.
The surgeon performs an adrenalectomy, removing the adrenal gland located above the kidney, prior to detaching the kidney at its superior pole.
The surgeon performs a non-block intrafacial resection of the kidney and surrounding ganglion tissue, carefully excising these structures from their anatomical attachments.
The excised tissues are placed into a retrieval bag and extracted through a pre-existing sub-umbilical midline incision.
